page: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8124M processors. It features 72 vCPUs across 36 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 2.67 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

A key differentiator of the c5n.metal instance is its 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, designed for network-intensive workloads. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 67384.33 and a PassMark CPU score of 50868.09. Memory performance is also highly rated, demonstrating a read-only large block memory bandwidth of 212.97 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 90.73 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the top tier, reaching 60.19 million operations per second.

While the server excels in multi-threaded compute, memory bandwidth, and network throughput, it exhibits limitations in single-core tasks and specific workloads. Single-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, fall into the average tier. Furthermore, without GPU acceleration, LLM text generation performance is poor, though prompt processing speeds remain average to strong. Given these characteristics, c5n.metal is highly efficient for high-performance computing, network appliances, distributed database nodes, and multi-threaded batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 72 vCPUs across 36 physical Intel Xeon 8124M cores running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and lacks local storage or GPUs, relying on its high-speed 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database throughput, as evidenced by its top-tier Redis and PassMark database benchmark results. However, single-core performance remains average, and LLM text generation is poor due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This profile makes c5n.metal highly suitable for network-intensive applications, distributed databases, high-performance computing, and multi-threaded batch processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal compute-optimized server with 72 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance for network-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal server with 72 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps bandwidth, optimized for high-compute and network workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal compute platform featuring high-speed DDR4 memory, massive multi-core processing power, and 100 Gbps baseline network throughput.
bullet_points: 72 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8124M processors at 3.4 GHz
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for distributed databases, HPC, and network appliances
